about.headline: Technical Communication Expert
about.bio: I am a Technical Writer and Documentation Architect with over eight years of experience turning dense engineering concepts into precise, accessible documentation. From REST and GraphQL APIs to SDKs, user manuals, and knowledge bases, I build documentation systems that help developers ship faster and reduce support load. My work blends editorial rigor with an intimate understanding of software architecture.
about.philosophy: Documentation is a product, not an afterthought. I believe every reader deserves a clear path from confusion to confidence. Good documentation is measured not by how much is written, but by how little the user has to think.

achievements: Authored documentation for 30+ public REST and GraphQL APIs.
Reduced developer onboarding time by 42% through restructured SDK guides.
Built a docs-as-code pipeline adopted across 6 engineering teams.
Documented a HIPAA-compliant healthcare platform used by 200+ clinics.
Created 120+ knowledge base articles, cutting ticket volume by 35%.
Standardized terminology with a company-wide style guide.
Produced end-user manuals for two enterprise SaaS products.
Owned the release-notes process across quarterly launches.

portfolio.title: Payments Platform API Reference
portfolio.description: A complete reference for a global payments API, including authentication, endpoints, and webhooks.
portfolio.content:
# Payments Platform API

This reference documents the public REST API for the Payments Platform. All requests are made to `https://api.payments.example.com/v1`.

## Authentication

Authenticate using a bearer token in the `Authorization` header.

```bash
curl -X GET https://api.payments.example.com/v1/charges \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"
```

## Create a Charge

```json
{
  "amount": 2000,
  "currency": "aud",
  "source": "tok_visa"
}
```

## Webhooks

Register a webhook endpoint to receive asynchronous event notifications such as `charge.succeeded`.

portfolio.title: IoT Device SDK Integration Guide
portfolio.description: Developer guide for an embedded IoT SDK, covering provisioning, telemetry, and OTA updates.
portfolio.content:
# IoT Device SDK

The Device SDK lets you connect hardware to the cloud in minutes.

## Installation

```bash
pip install iot-device-sdk
```

## Connect a Device

```python
from iot_sdk import Device

device = Device(token="YOUR_TOKEN")
device.connect()
device.publish("temperature", 22.5)
```

## Over-the-Air Updates

Devices poll the update service every 60 seconds and apply signed firmware automatically.

portfolio.title: Analytics Dashboard User Manual
portfolio.description: An end-user manual for a product analytics dashboard, from first login to advanced cohorts.
portfolio.content:
# Analytics Dashboard

Welcome to the Analytics Dashboard. This guide walks you through every core feature.

## Getting Started

1. Log in with your workspace credentials.
2. Connect a data source from **Settings > Sources**.
3. Open the **Overview** tab to see live metrics.

## Building a Cohort

Use the Cohorts builder to segment users by behavior. Save cohorts to reuse them in reports.

portfolio.title: Platform v3.0 Release Notes
portfolio.description: Structured release notes for a major platform version, highlighting breaking changes.
portfolio.content:
# Platform v3.0 Release Notes

Released: 2025-01-15

## What's New

- New GraphQL gateway with persisted queries.
- Redesigned billing console.

## Breaking Changes

- Removed the legacy `/v0` endpoints. Migrate to `/v1`.
- `amount` is now an integer in minor currency units.

## Fixed

- Resolved pagination drift on the `/events` endpoint.

portfolio.title: Troubleshooting Knowledge Base
portfolio.description: A searchable knowledge base of 60+ troubleshooting articles for a SaaS platform.
portfolio.content:
# Troubleshooting: Login Failures

## Symptom

Users see `Invalid credentials` despite a correct password.

## Causes

1. Expired session token.
2. SSO misconfiguration.

## Resolution

- Clear browser cache and re-authenticate.
- Verify the SSO ACS URL matches the workspace settings.

portfolio.title: GraphQL for Backend Engineers
portfolio.description: A multi-part tutorial teaching backend engineers to design and document GraphQL schemas.
portfolio.content:
# GraphQL for Backend Engineers

## Defining a Schema

```graphql
type Query {
  user(id: ID!): User
}

type User {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  posts: [Post!]!
}
```

## Documenting Resolvers

Pair each field with a description so your auto-generated docs stay accurate.

blog.title: Writing API Docs Developers Actually Love
blog.excerpt: The difference between documentation that gets used and documentation that gets ignored comes down to a few deliberate choices.
blog.content: Great API documentation respects the developer's time. Start with a copy-pasteable quickstart, show real responses, and never hide errors. In this post we explore a repeatable structure for API references that reduces support tickets and accelerates integration.

blog.title: Docs-as-Code in Practice
blog.excerpt: Treating documentation like software unlocks review, versioning, and automation you can trust.
blog.content: Docs-as-code means writing in Markdown, storing it in Git, and publishing through a pipeline. This post covers the tooling, branching model, and CI checks that make a docs-as-code workflow sustainable for small and large teams alike.

blog.title: The Documentation Toolchain I Rely On in 2025
blog.excerpt: A practical tour of the tools that power a modern technical writing workflow.
blog.content: From Docusaurus to MadCap Flare, the documentation toolchain keeps evolving. Here is the stack I reach for when starting a new docs project, and why each tool earns its place.

blog.title: Where Technical Writing Is Heading
blog.excerpt: AI, interactive docs, and developer experience are reshaping our discipline. Here is what to watch.
blog.content: Technical writing is shifting from static pages to interactive, personalized experiences. We look at how AI-assisted authoring and embedded live examples are changing expectations for documentation teams.
